key_findings.most_important_params: max_grad_norm (35.3%) and gamma (26.8%) dominate PPO performance
key_findings.arch_256_essential: All positive-Sharpe trials used hidden_size=256; 64/128 consistently failed
key_findings.higher_lr_works: Optuna best lr=3.47e-4, much higher than FinRL/ElegantRL literature suggests
key_findings.wider_clipping: clip_range=0.3 outperformed tighter 0.1/0.2 — opposite of initial hypothesis

literature_justification.learning_rate: FinRL: 2.5e-4; ElegantRL: 3e-5. Optuna found 3.5e-4 — higher than literature for this env.
literature_justification.gamma: FinRL default: 0.99. Optuna found 0.982 — slightly lower discount works better.
literature_justification.clip_range: Schulman 2017: 0.2. Optuna found 0.3 — wider clipping better for portfolio task.
literature_justification.n_steps: FinRL: 2048. Optuna found 512 — shorter rollouts with more updates.
literature_justification.ent_coef: FinRL: 0.01. Optuna found 8e-4 — much lower; focused policy preferred.
literature_justification.gae_lambda: Schulman 2016 GAE: 0.95. Optuna found 0.976 — higher for this env.
literature_justification.total_timesteps: FinRL: 100K+. MDPI 2024: 10M. Optuna: 400K optimal for 50-asset tuning subset.
literature_justification.vec_normalize: Always ON — '37 Impl Details' (ICLR 2022): most impactful PPO trick.
literature_justification.network: [256,256] with Tanh activation — matches '37 Impl Details' recommendation.

sources: 37 Implementation Details of PPO (ICLR Blog Track 2022)
SB3 RL Baselines Zoo — hyperparameter optimization module
Schulman et al. (2017) — Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
Schulman et al. (2016) — High-Dimensional Continuous Control Using GAE
MDPI Algorithms 2024 — Systematic Portfolio Optimization with RL
